_source: NCAA D1 basketball analytics estimates (Kubatko et al., adjusted for Ivy League pace/style). Calibrate to Ivy-specific play-by-play once available.
_unit: Expected points scored by the possession-holding team from this game state
_ivy_note: Ivy League values skew slightly lower than D1 average (less athleticism, fewer transition buckets). These are conservative estimates.

transition_live_steal.label: Transition — live ball steal
transition_live_steal.note: Opponent has numbers advantage; higher EP than half-court

transition_def_rebound.label: Transition — defensive rebound
transition_def_rebound.note: Outlet pass opportunity; slightly above half-court

dead_ball_inbound.label: Dead ball inbound
dead_ball_inbound.note: Opponent sets defense; same as half-court set

putback_attempt.label: Offensive rebound — putback
putback_attempt.note: Close-range attempt in crowd; high conversion rate

reset_possession.label: Offensive rebound — reset
reset_possession.note: ORB retreated to perimeter; slightly below fresh half-court

foul_drawn_two_shots.label: Two free throws
foul_drawn_two_shots.note: Based on ~72% Ivy FT% (2 × 0.72 = 1.44, minus possession risk)

foul_drawn_one_and_one.label: One-and-one
foul_drawn_one_and_one.note: Based on ~72% Ivy FT% (0.72 + 0.72² = 1.24 pts, minus risk)

event_state_map._note: Maps each Four Factor event to the possession states it typically creates for each team

offTurnover.interpretation: 37% of turnovers are live-ball steals; 63% dead ball. Weighted opponent EP = 1.12.

offRebound.interpretation: 42% of ORBs lead to immediate putback attempts; 58% reset. Weighted own EP = 1.00.

defForcedTurnover.interpretation: Mirror of offTurnover — live steals give your team transition; dead balls give half-court.
